findings.evidence: `rg -n "transpose|relabelRows|relabelColumns|swapRows|swapColumns" TauCeti/KnotTheory/Grid/Diagram.lean` shows transpose lemmas only at lines 317-338 and no transpose/relabel or transpose/swap interaction lemmas; existing symmetry API includes `GridState.relabelRows_relabelColumns` at line 237 and swap membership/involution lemmas at lines 284-305.
findings.fix: Add the row/column exchange API, e.g. `(x.relabelRows ρ).transpose = x.transpose.relabelColumns ρ` and `(x.relabelColumns κ).transpose = x.transpose.relabelRows κ`, plus the corresponding swap specializations if they are intended as first-class APIs.
findings.issue: `GridState.transpose` has no public lemmas describing how it interacts with `relabelRows`/`relabelColumns` or `swapRows`/`swapColumns`, even though these are the surrounding symmetry operations.

findings.evidence: The diagram transpose API at lines 649-687 provides component, involution, set-image, and membership lemmas only; the surrounding diagram symmetry API already exposes composition/commutation at lines 597-633.
findings.fix: Add diagram-level versions such as `(G.relabelRows ρ).transpose = G.transpose.relabelColumns ρ` and `(G.relabelColumns κ).transpose = G.transpose.relabelRows κ`, with swap forms if users are expected to work through `swapRows`/`swapColumns`.
findings.issue: `GridDiagram.transpose` likewise omits the public row/column exchange lemmas for diagram relabelings and swaps.

summary: The transpose surface has the basic membership and grading invariance lemmas, but it is missing characteristic interaction lemmas with the row/column symmetry operations already in scope. Without those, downstream symmetry users must unfold `transpose` and the relabeling definitions to compose the public symmetries.
